skill.name: biodbnet-api
skill.description: Access bioDBnet REST services for biological identifier conversion, pathway retrieval, and ortholog mapping. Use when you need to convert gene/protein IDs, find pathways, or retrieve biological annotations via bioDBnet.
skill.summary: Access bioDBnet REST services for biological identifier conversion, pathway retrieval, and ortholog mapping.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 123 static analysis findings were reviewed and dismissed as false positives. The weak cryptographic algorithm findings reference standard HTTPS connections to the legitimate bioDBnet.gov server, which is correct and expected behavior. The shell backtick execution findings are markdown code block delimiters in documentation files showing example API usage, not executable code. System reconnaissance findings are from the scanner's own output file, not from skill code. This skill is a legitimate bioinformatics API client that makes REST calls to the public bioDBnet service for biological identifier conversion and pathway retrieval. No malicious intent, data exfiltration, or prompt injection was detected.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak cryptographic algorithm reference
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged HTTPS URLs as weak cryptographic algorithm. These are standard HTTPS URLs pointing to the legitimate bioDBnet server at biodbnet-abcc.ncifcrf.gov. HTTPS/TLS is the correct secure protocol for API communication. No actual weak crypto is used.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Shell backtick execution in documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged markdown code block backticks as Ruby shell execution. The files references/api_methods.md and SKILL.md are documentation files, not Ruby scripts. The backticks are markdown formatting for code examples showing example curl and python commands. No actual command execution occurs from these files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System reconnaissance in audit data
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged system reconnaissance patterns in biodbnet-api_audit_result_v2.json. This file is the static scanner's own output file containing audit results. The flagged content is metadata about the scan itself, not executable code or actual reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URL to legitimate API endpoint
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged hardcoded URLs pointing to biodbnet-abcc.ncifcrf.gov. This is the legitimate public bioDBnet REST API endpoint. The URLs appear in documentation and as the BASE_URL constant in the Python client, which is the intended target of this skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Python HTTP library usage
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged requests.get() usage as a network risk. This is the core intended functionality of the bioDBnet API client. The script makes legitimate REST API calls to a public bioinformatics web service. No unauthorized or suspicious network activity.

content.user_title: Query bioDBnet for biological identifier conversion and pathway analysis
content.value_statement: This skill connects to the bioDBnet REST API to convert biological identifiers across multiple databases. It retrieves gene pathways, finds orthologs between species, and gets biological annotations with simple structured commands.
actual_capabilities: Convert biological identifiers between databases such as gene symbols to Entrez Gene IDs or Affy IDs
Retrieve signaling pathways for a given species using taxon ID
Find orthologs between different species for comparative genomics
Retrieve biological annotations for genes, proteins, drugs, diseases, and GO terms
Perform graph walks through biological database relationship networks
Discover intermediate nodes between biological inputs and outputs
limitations: Requires internet access to reach the bioDBnet API server at NCIFCRF
Only supports methods and parameters exposed by the bioDBnet REST API
Cannot analyze private biological data not accessible through bioDBnet
Response format and available data depend on bioDBnet service availability

use_cases.title: Convert gene identifiers across databases
use_cases.description: A researcher has a list of gene IDs from one database and needs them converted to another format for analysis in a different tool.

use_cases.title: Retrieve biological pathways for a species
use_cases.description: A biologist needs to find all available signaling pathways for human or another species using the bioDBnet pathway service.

use_cases.title: Map orthologs between species
use_cases.description: A scientist studying comparative genomics needs to find orthologous genes between two species to identify conserved biological functions.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a single gene identifier
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert the gene symbol TP53 to its Entrez Gene ID using the bioDBnet API.

prompt_templates.title: Batch convert multiple gene identifiers
prompt_templates.prompt: I have these gene symbols: TP53, BRCA1, EGFR, MYC. Convert all of them to their Entrez Gene IDs and Affy IDs.
prompt_templates.scenario: Batch identifier conversion for downstream analysis

prompt_templates.title: Find pathways for a species
prompt_templates.prompt: Get all available pathways for human (taxon ID 9606) using the bioDBnet pathway service. Show the pathway names and their sources.
prompt_templates.scenario: Pathway analysis for a target organism

prompt_templates.title: Multi-species ortholog mapping
prompt_templates.prompt: Find orthologs for the human gene BRCA1 in mouse. Use bioDBnet to map from human gene symbol to mouse ortholog.

output_examples.input: Convert the gene symbol TP53 to its Entrez Gene ID
output_examples.output: The human gene TP53 (Tumor Protein P53) maps to Entrez Gene ID 7157. This is a tumor suppressor gene commonly studied in cancer research.
BioDBnet returned 15 available pathway sources for Homo sapiens including KEGG, NCIPID, BioCarta, and Reactome. The total number of pathway entries available across all sources is 312.

output_examples.input: Find mouse orthologs for human BRCA1 gene
output_examples.output: The human BRCA1 gene maps to the mouse ortholog Brca1. Both genes share conserved functions in DNA repair and tumor suppression.

best_practices: Always specify the correct taxon ID (e.g., 9606 for human) to get accurate species-specific results from the API
Start with the getInputs method to discover available input types before running identifier conversions
Verify that input identifier types match the expected format for the chosen bioDBnet method to avoid empty results
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to map identifiers across unrelated biological databases without first checking compatibility using getOutputsForInput
Do not ignore error responses from the API - malformed input values or invalid parameters will return empty or error results
Do not use this skill for real-time clinical decision support without validating the data through primary biological sources

faq.question: What is bioDBnet?
faq.answer: bioDBnet is a web service that connects multiple biological databases to provide identifier conversion, pathway retrieval, and annotation lookup.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No, the bioDBnet REST API is publicly accessible and does not require any authentication or API key.

faq.question: What types of biological identifiers can I convert?
faq.answer: You can convert between gene symbols, Entrez Gene IDs, Affy IDs, UniProt IDs, RefSeq IDs, and many other identifier types.

faq.question: What species are supported by bioDBnet?
faq.answer: BioDBnet supports many species. Each species has a numeric taxon ID such as 9606 for human and 10090 for mouse.

faq.question: How do I find the list of available API methods?
faq.answer: The skill includes a reference document with all supported methods. You can also call the getInputs method to discover available input types.

faq.question: What format does the API output come in?
faq.answer: The bioDBnet API returns JSON data. The skill formats this as readable structured output for further use.
